dataset: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001
case_id: S001R01 (Eyes Open Baseline) and S001R04 (Motor Imagery)

pi_items.source: GitHub release page https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2 confirms v5.2.2 exists and was released August 21, 2024
GUI Widget Guide documentation and forum discussions confirm OpenBCI GUI has configurable bandpass filter

Multiple filter options exist (0.5-15, 5-50, 7-13, 15-50, 1-50, 1-100 Hz) but authoritative sources conflict: NeuroBB forum discussion with Chip Audette (filter code author) indicates default may be 1-50 Hz, while one search result claimed 5-50 Hz default. Official OpenBCI documentation does not specify the startup default value.
Task description and setup script confirm task goal is to change filter to 1-50 Hz; this is a valid filter option in OpenBCI GUI
Difficulty is subjective and not objectively verifiable from documentation. Task requires finding filter control, opening dialog, and modifying one parameter.

privileged_info_summary: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is confirmed to exist and include configurable bandpass filter options. The task uses real EEG data from PhysioNet EEGMMIDB dataset (Subject S001, Runs 01 and 04), converted from 64-channel 160Hz EDF format to 8-channel 250Hz OpenBCI CSV format. The GUI supports multiple bandpass filter ranges including 1-50 Hz and 5-50 Hz. Task requires modifying filter start frequency from initial value to 1.0 Hz.
